summary: This brief post announces a new Bloomberg Opinion column about facial recognition technology being used by Chinese insurance companies. O'Neil was motivated to write after becoming alarmed by developments in China's use of AI for health insurance decisions. The post serves primarily as a pointer to her longer Bloomberg piece, which examines how Chinese insurers are using facial recognition as a cautionary example for American readers. She frames the Chinese case as a preview of what could happen to health insurance in the U.S.
key_points: Chinese insurers are deploying facial recognition technology in ways that affect health insurance access
O'Neil views these developments as alarming enough to break from a writing hiatus
The Chinese case serves as a warning for potential similar developments in U.S. health insurance
AI and facial recognition are being weaponized against consumers in insurance contexts
money_quotes: I got so riled up about some incredibly terrible facial recognition stuff going on in China that I wrote a Bloomberg piece
China Knows How to Take Away Your Health Insurance
A big Chinese insurer's facial-recognition software offers a cautionary tale for the U.S.
key_insight: China's deployment of facial recognition in health insurance decisions previews a dystopian future that Americans should actively work to prevent.
